prompt: Create a cinematic widescreen night scene in the style of an intimate romantic indie film, similar to a quiet conversation moment from Before Sunrise. On a city sidewalk at night, show exactly two young adults facing each other at a slight distance: a man on the left wearing a light beige button-up shirt with sleeves rolled up and dark trousers, one hand in his pocket, and a woman on the right wearing a simple black sleeveless dress, her dark hair tied back, hands loosely clasped near her waist. Use a modern downtown street with high-rise buildings in the background, with glowing office windows, distant traffic, blurred car headlights and taillights, and warm orange streetlights reflecting on the pavement. Compose as a wide 16:9 film still, both characters in profile or three-quarter silhouette, separated by an empty walkway leading into the depth of the frame. Lighting should be low-key and atmospheric: warm amber light from the left streetlamp, cooler blue light from a building on the right, soft bokeh, shallow depth of field, natural shadows, subdued contrast, realistic skin tones, and a moody late-night urban palette. Make it look like a real live-action movie frame shot on an anamorphic lens, with cinematic grain, soft focus falloff, no visible text, no watermark, no extra people in the foreground. Parameterize the mood as quiet, tentative, romantic tension, the man as young Asian man in a beige shirt, the woman as young Asian woman in a black dress, and the camera feel as naturalistic handheld indie film still.
